Patch Set 29:

There are a few cases where the commit callback might affect performance. 

If a client is newly mounted, then operations on the export will be synchronous until the export is committed to the last_rcvd file. If the callback does not run for some time it might cause a bunch of metadata operations to wait for transaction commit?

Another possibility is if the callback is needed for the multi-slot last_rcvd feature. If RPCs are not being marked committed it might prevent clients from keeping enough RPCs in flight?

It probably makes sense to see where commit callbacks are used, and then register a real transaction commit callback for time-critical operations, and use the batched callback for last_committed updates and such?

Andreas, the commit callback optimized in this patch is local to OSP and it's used only to initiate OST_DESTROY and OST_SETATTR (including llog records cancelation)

Patch Set 36:

> sorry, I don't understand. this patch doesn't call commit callback,
 > it just register _own_ commit callback every second, for internal
 > (to OSP) purpose. please clarify.

as far as I can understand, the current implementation registers commit callback every second. That means the committed ID will be delayed to update, by half second on average. For example, when a SETATTR record to OST is added into llog, a commit callback is scheduled in one second, even though the llog trans is committed immediately, the SETATTR RPC to OST will still be delayed up to one second.

This may be okay for regular SETATTR or DESTROY request, but it can cause problems for FLR. In FLR implementation, the MDT has to transfer the layout version to OST objects via SETATTR RPC, and client will carry the same layout version to make writes succeed. Due to the delay I mentioned above, the OST won't get the desired layout version on time, therefore the client has to wait up to one second before it can make a successful write.

Please let me know if you have any further questions.

commit.subject: LU-7251 osp: do not assign commit callback to every thandle
commit.message:
LU-7251 osp: do not assign commit callback to every thandle

with OSP there is a risk of getting a lot of commit callbacks.
say, 10K unlinks/sec on 4-striped files could result in 4*10K*5
= 200K commit callbacks. this patch implements another schema:
every OSP registers own callback every second. this should result
in 4*5 commit callbacks in the same situation. in case of forced
sync the commit callback is registered unconditionally.

the patch removes th_tags and th_ctx from struct thandle as they
are not used anymore. this elimintates 3 allocations from every
transaction:
(lu_object.c:1714:keys_init()) kmalloced 'ctx->lc_value': 320
(update_records.c:1217:update_key_init()) kmalloced 'value': 408
(osp_dev.c:1807:osp_txn_key_init()) kmalloced 'value': 4
